title: Contributing to polyglotSQL
text: need R (>= 4.2), Rust (cargo + rustc >= 1.88) usual R dev packages (devtools, rextendr, testthat, pkgdown). Rust sources live src/rust/. changing exported #[extendr] functions, rebuild R/extendr-wrappers.R regenerated (written cargo run --bin document build). Run cargo fmt cargo clippy --lib inside src/rust/ pushing; CI enforces .
code: devtools::document()   # regenerates Rd + NAMESPACE (and Rust wrappers on build) devtools::test() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to polyglotSQL
text: script bumps dependency src/rust/Cargo.toml, refreshes Cargo.lock, re-vendors crates src/rust/vendor.tar.xz (archive used offline builds), regenerates inst/COPYRIGHTS. : update Config/polyglotSQL/upstream DESCRIPTION; run test suite (devtools::test()), asserts linked crate version matches DESCRIPTION; mention upgrade NEWS.md.
code: tools/update-vendor.sh <new-version>

title: Contributing to polyglotSQL
text: pkgdown workflow builds site every push main (manually via Actions → pkgdown → Run workflow) pushes rendered site gh-pages branch using repository GITHUB_TOKEN. enable per repository: Settings → Pages → Build deployment → Source: “Deploy branch” → Branch: gh-pages / / (root). first workflow run creates branch automatically. Local preview: pkgdown::build_site() open docs/index.html.

title: Migrating SQL between dialects
text: Function names, quoting syntax differ per engine. polyglotSQL rewrites structurally:
code: # MySQL constructs -> PostgreSQL sql_transpile(   "SELECT IFNULL(a, b), DATE_FORMAT(d, '%Y-%m-%d') FROM t LIMIT 5",   from = "mysql", to = "postgres" ) #> [1] "SELECT COALESCE(a, b), TO_CHAR(d, '%Y-%m-%d') FROM t LIMIT 5"  # T-SQL pagination and date functions -> PostgreSQL sql_transpile(   "SELECT TOP 10 name, GETDATE() AS now FROM users ORDER BY name",   from = "tsql", to = "postgres" ) #> [1] "SELECT name, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS now FROM users ORDER BY name NULLS FIRST LIMIT 10"  # BigQuery -> Snowflake: quoting and safe casts sql_transpile(   "SELECT `user id`, SAFE_CAST(x AS INT64) FROM `proj.dataset.tbl`",   from = "bigquery", to = "snowflake" ) #> [1] "SELECT \"user id\", CAST(x AS BIGINT) FROM \"proj.dataset.tbl\""

title: Migrating SQL between dialects
text: inputs outputs plain character vectors, migrating whole directory vapply():
code: queries <- c(   orders  = "SELECT IFNULL(status, 'unknown') AS status FROM orders",   daily   = "SELECT DATE(created_at) AS d, COUNT(*) FROM events GROUP BY DATE(created_at)",   users   = "SELECT id, CONCAT(first, ' ', last) AS full_name FROM users" )  vapply(queries, sql_transpile, character(1),        from = "mysql", to = "duckdb") #>                                                                                         orders  #>                                     "SELECT COALESCE(status, 'unknown') AS status FROM orders"  #>                                                                                          daily  #> "SELECT CAST(created_at AS DATE) AS d, COUNT(*) FROM events GROUP BY CAST(created_at AS DATE)"  #>                                                                                          users  #>                                  "SELECT id, CONCAT(first, ' ', last) AS full_name FROM users"

title: Migrating SQL between dialects
text: default unsupported = "raise", polyglotSQL refuses emit SQL target dialect express construct, raising polyglot_transpile_error. inventory pass may prefer collect failures: unsupported = "warn" "ignore" instead return closest supported translation — useful first draft human reviews.
code: migrate <- function(sql, from, to) {   tryCatch(     list(ok = TRUE, sql = sql_transpile(sql, from = from, to = to)),     polyglot_error = function(e) list(ok = FALSE, error = conditionMessage(e))   ) } migrate("SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t", "mysql", "postgres") #> $ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $sql #> [1] "SELECT COALESCE(a, b) FROM t"

title: Migrating SQL between dialects
text: sql_diff() shows actually changed two statements, handy reviewing rewrites: sql_validate() confirms output parses target dialect:
code: sql_diff(   "SELECT a FROM t",   "SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE a > 1" ) #>       op expression target #> 1 insert          a   <NA> #> 2 insert          b   <NA> #> 3 insert      a > 1   <NA> #> 4 insert          1   <NA> #> 5   move          a   <NA> out <- sql_transpile("SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t", from = "mysql", to = "postgres") sql_validate(out, dialect = "postgres")$valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Migrating SQL between dialects
text: Transpilation operates syntax. : emulate engine-specific semantics (collations, implicit casts, NULL ordering, integer vs. float division, timezone behavior); create missing functions target engine; guarantee identical performance characteristics. Run translated queries target database — ideally result comparisons — trusting production.

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
text: flagship feature dialect translation. SQL parsed source dialect abstract syntax tree (AST) regenerated target dialect’s rules: Multiple statements supported; result one element per statement: Set pretty = TRUE indented output, control happens construct equivalent target dialect unsupported ("raise" — default — errors; "warn"/"ignore" return best-effort SQL):
code: sql_transpile(   "SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t",   from = "mysql",   to = "postgres" ) #> [1] "SELECT COALESCE(a, b) FROM t" sql_transpile(   "SELECT 1; SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t;",   from = "mysql",   to = "postgres" ) #> [1] "SELECT 1"                     "SELECT COALESCE(a, b) FROM t" cat(sql_transpile(   "SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM logs GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 10",   from = "generic", to = "snowflake", pretty = TRUE )) #> SELECT #>   id, #>   COUNT(*) AS n #> FROM logs #> GROUP BY #>   id #> HAVING #>   COUNT(*) > 10

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
text: function accepting dialect also accepts listed aliases — "mssql" "sqlserver" mean "tsql", "postgresql" means "postgres".
code: head(sql_dialects(full = TRUE), 10) #>          name  aliases                                    description #> 1     generic          Standard SQL with no dialect-specific behavior #> 2  postgresql postgres                                     PostgreSQL #> 3       mysql                                                   MySQL #> 4    bigquery                                         Google BigQuery #> 5   snowflake                                               Snowflake #> 6      duckdb                                                  DuckDB #> 7      sqlite                                                  SQLite #> 8        hive                                             Apache Hive #> 9       spark   spark2                               Apache Spark SQL #> 10      trino                              Trino (formerly PrestoSQL)

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
code: cat(sql_format("select id,sum(x) total from t where y=1 group by id")) #> SELECT #>   id, #>   SUM(x) AS total #> FROM t #> WHERE #>   y = 1 #> GROUP BY #>   id

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
text: sql_validate() returns structured result instead throwing:
code: sql_validate("SELECT FROM WHERE") #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E003] Expected table name or subquery, got Where at 1:18

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
text: AST plain nested list following upstream JSON format, can rendered back SQL dialect’s syntax rules (full translation function rewrites, use sql_transpile()):
code: ast <- sql_parse("SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1") ast #> <polyglot_ast> 1 statement (generic) #> [1] select sql_generate(sql_parse("SELECT `col name` FROM t", dialect = "mysql"),              dialect = "postgres") #> [1] "SELECT \"col name\" FROM t"

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
text: failures raise ordinary R conditions useful classes (polyglot_parse_error, polyglot_transpile_error, polyglot_validation_error, polyglot_guard_error, inheriting polyglot_error), can handle precisely: parse failure — even bug-triggered panic inside Rust — never terminates R session.
code: tryCatch(   sql_parse("SELECT ((( FROM"),   polyglot_parse_error = function(e) conditionMessage(e) ) #> [1] "Expected table name or subquery, got From (line 1, column 16)"

title: Getting started with polyglotSQL
text: vignette("dialect-migration") — migrating query base engines. vignette("parsing-validation-lineage") — ASTs, schemas, lineage analysis. vignette("installation--troubleshooting") — Rust toolchain, offline builds, common problems.

title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: polyglotSQL compiled Rust backend. install source need: R >= 4.2 Rust: cargo rustc >= 1.88 xz utility (unpack vendored dependencies; present virtually every system) Rust needed installing prebuilt binary (e.g. CI artifacts binary repository).

title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: recommended path every platform rustup: Alternatives: brew install rust (macOS), apt-get install cargo rustc (Debian/Ubuntu — check version!), official Windows installer https://forge.rust-lang.org/infra/-installation-methods.html. Windows, gnu toolchain targeting x86_64-pc-windows-gnu required R; rustup installs : package’s configure script checks toolchain front prints exactly missing (never installs anything ).
code: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh rustup target add x86_64-pc-windows-gnu

title: Installation and troubleshooting
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("StrategicProjects/polyglot-sql-r")

title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: source package ships Rust dependencies vendored archive (src/rust/vendor.tar.xz), build runs cargo --offline. Nothing downloaded R CMD INSTALL — internet connection needed obtain package .

what: Build details you may care about
title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: Compilation uses 2 parallel jobs (CRAN policy). Rust static library linked single shared object; installed package runtime dependency cargo. DEBUG=true R CMD INSTALL . produces debug build (faster compile, slower runtime).

what: cargo: command not found / configure fails
title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: Rust PATH R uses. installing via rustup, restart R ~/.cargo/bin (added shell profile) visible, add manually:
code: Sys.setenv(PATH = paste0(Sys.getenv("PATH"), ":", path.expand("~/.cargo/bin")))

title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: Distribution packages often old. Install via rustup, update rustup update stable.

title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: Install Xcode command-line tools: xcode-select --install.

what: Windows: cannot find -lpolyglotsql or wrong target
title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: Make sure x86_64-pc-windows-gnu target installed (see ) using Rtools matching R version.

title: Installation and troubleshooting
text: upstream crate version pinned src/rust/Cargo.toml Cargo.lock, sources vendored. upgrade: update Config/polyglotSQL/upstream DESCRIPTION, run test suite, check polyglot_version() reports new version.
code: tools/update-vendor.sh 0.6.3   # bumps polyglot-sql, re-vendors, re-packs

title: Installation and troubleshooting
code: library(polyglotSQL) polyglot_version() sql_transpile("SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t", from = "mysql", to = "postgres")

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: sql_parse() returns full abstract syntax tree nested R lists, following upstream JSON AST format: AST round-trips: sql_generate() renders back SQL dialect.
code: ast <- sql_parse("SELECT a, SUM(b) AS total FROM t GROUP BY a") ast #> <polyglot_ast> 1 statement (generic) #> [1] select str(ast$statements[[1]], max.level = 3, list.len = 4) #> List of 1 #>  $ select:List of 26 #>   ..$ cluster_by      : NULL #>   ..$ connect         : NULL #>   ..$ distinct        : logi FALSE #>   ..$ distinct_on     : NULL #>   .. [list output truncated]

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: lower-level tooling (syntax highlighting, linters), sql_tokenize() exposes token stream exact positions:
code: sql_tokenize("SELECT a FROM t WHERE x = 'hé'") #> <polyglot_tokens> 8 tokens #>     type   text line column start end #> 1 Select SELECT    1      7     0   6 #> 2    Var      a    1      9     7   8 #> 3   From   FROM    1     14     9  13 #> 4    Var      t    1     16    14  15 #> 5  Where  WHERE    1     22    16  21 #> 6    Var      x    1     24    22  23 #> 7     Eq      =    1     26    24  25 #> 8 String     hé    1     31    26  30

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: Three layers checking available: third layer schema-aware validation. Describe tables named list — names tables, values (optionally named) column vectors: Use error = TRUE turn invalid result polyglot_validation_error condition — convenient pipelines.
code: # 1. Syntax only (default) sql_validate("SELECT FROM WHERE") #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E003] Expected table name or subquery, got Where at 1:18  # 2. Strict syntax + semantic lint warnings sql_validate("SELECT name, FROM employees", strict_syntax = TRUE) #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E005] Trailing comma before FROM is not allowed in strict syntax mode at 1:13 sql_validate("SELECT *, category FROM products LIMIT 10", semantic = TRUE) #> <polyglot_validation> valid (generic) #> [W001] SELECT * is discouraged; specify columns explicitly for better #> performance and maintainability #> [W004] LIMIT without ORDER BY produces non-deterministic results schema <- list(   orders = c(o_id = "INT", o_user = "INT", o_total = "DECIMAL(10,2)"),   users  = c(id = "INT", name = "TEXT") )  sql_validate("SELECT o_missing FROM orders", schema = schema) #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E201] Unknown column 'o_missing' in table 'orders'

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: Note CTE listed — physical sources .
code: sql_source_tables(   "WITH cte AS (SELECT id FROM base)    SELECT * FROM cte JOIN other USING (id)" ) #> [1] "other" "base"

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: sql_lineage() traces every output column CTEs, subqueries expressions source tables: entry carries full lineage tree: schema improves resolution unqualified ambiguous columns, column = restricts lineage one output column.
code: lin <- sql_lineage(   "WITH base AS (SELECT id, amount FROM payments)    SELECT id, amount * 2 AS doubled FROM base" ) lin #> <polyglot_lineage> 2 columns (generic) #> id ← payments #> doubled ← payments str(lin$columns[[2]]$tree, max.level = 2) #> List of 5 #>  $ downstream :List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 5 #>  $ expression : chr "amount * 2 AS doubled" #>  $ name       : chr "doubled" #>  $ source_kind: chr "Root" #>  $ source_name: chr ""

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: sql_analyze() condenses query facts — shape, projections, relations, CTEs, set operations:
code: a <- sql_analyze(   "WITH x AS (SELECT id FROM t)    SELECT x.id, UPPER(name) AS shout FROM x JOIN u ON x.id = u.id" ) a #> <polyglot_analysis> shape: select (generic) #> projections: id, shout #> relations: u, x #> ctes: x vapply(a$projections, function(p) p$transformKind, character(1)) #> [1] "direct"     "expression"

title: Parsing, validation and lineage
text: data catalogs speak OpenLineage, sql_openlineage() emits columnLineage facet inferred input/output datasets:
code: ol <- sql_openlineage(   "INSERT INTO reports SELECT id, total FROM sales",   namespace = "warehouse" ) names(ol) #> [1] "facet"    "inputs"   "outputs"  "warnings"

what: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
text: polyglotSQL parses, tokenizes, validates, formats, analyzes translates SQL 30+ dialects — PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, DuckDB, SQLite, T-SQL, Oracle, ClickHouse, Trino, Spark many — directly R. embeds polyglot-sql Rust crate (Rust port excellent SQLGlot Python library), everything runs natively -process: Python, Java, database connection, network calls.

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
text: Analytics teams rarely live single SQL dialect. Warehouses migrate (Redshift → Snowflake, anything → DuckDB), pipelines target several engines , auditing queries requires understanding structurally, strings. polyglotSQL gives R real SQL compiler front-end: proper parser, AST, dialect-aware generators, column-level lineage structural analysis. compares related R packages:

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
text: Binaries built CI need Rust toolchain. Installing source requires Rust (cargo rustc >= 1.88): : package builds fully offline: Rust dependencies vendored source tarball nothing downloaded R CMD INSTALL.
code: # install Rust once, via rustup: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("StrategicProjects/polyglot-sql-r")

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
text: default unsupported constructs raise classed error (unsupported = "raise"); use "warn" "ignore" force best-effort output.
code: library(polyglotSQL)  sql_transpile(   "SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t",   from = "mysql",   to = "postgres" ) #> [1] "SELECT COALESCE(a, b) FROM t"  sql_transpile(   "SELECT TOP 5 name, GETDATE() FROM users",   from = "tsql",   to = "duckdb" ) #> [1] "SELECT name, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP FROM users LIMIT 5"

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
code: cat(sql_format("select id,sum(x) total from t where y=1 group by id")) #> SELECT #>   id, #>   SUM(x) AS total #> FROM t #> WHERE #>   y = 1 #> GROUP BY #>   id

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
code: ast <- sql_parse("SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1") ast #> <polyglot_ast> 1 statement (generic) #> [1] select  sql_tokenize("SELECT a FROM t") #> <polyglot_tokens> 4 tokens #>     type   text line column start end #> 1 Select SELECT    1      7     0   6 #> 2    Var      a    1      9     7   8 #> 3   From   FROM    1     14     9  13 #> 4    Var      t    1     16    14  15

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
code: sql_validate("SELECT FROM WHERE") #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E003] Expected table name or subquery, got Where at 1:18  sql_validate(   "SELECT nonexistent FROM t",   schema = list(t = c(id = "INT", name = "TEXT")) ) #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E201] Unknown column 'nonexistent' in table 't'

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
code: sql_lineage(   "WITH base AS (SELECT id, amount FROM payments)    SELECT id, amount * 2 AS doubled FROM base" ) #> <polyglot_lineage> 2 columns (generic) #> id ← payments #> doubled ← payments

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
code: sql_dialects() #>  [1] "generic"     "postgresql"  "mysql"       "bigquery"    "snowflake"   #>  [6] "duckdb"      "sqlite"      "hive"        "spark"       "trino"       #> [11] "presto"      "redshift"    "tsql"        "oracle"      "clickhouse"  #> [16] "databricks"  "athena"      "teradata"    "doris"       "starrocks"   #> [21] "materialize" "risingwave"  "singlestore" "cockroachdb" "tidb"        #> [26] "druid"       "solr"        "tableau"     "dune"        "fabric"      #> [31] "drill"       "dremio"      "exasol"      "datafusion"

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
text: Transpilation syntactic best-effort. Two engines can accept query text still disagree implicit casts, collations, NULL ordering, integer division, time zones, function edge cases. polyglotSQL (like SQLGlot) emulate engine semantics. Always test transpiled SQL target database relying production.

title: polyglotSQL — SQL parsing, analysis and dialect translation for R
text: polyglotSQL (MIT) statically links polyglot-sql crate Tobias Müller (MIT), derives SQLGlot Toby Mao (MIT). Licenses vendored Rust crates collected inst/COPYRIGHTS.

what: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
title: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
text: Several polyglotSQL functions (sql_validate(), sql_lineage(), sql_analyze(), sql_optimize(), sql_annotate_types(), sql_openlineage()) accept optional schema argument describing tables referenced query. schema enables column qualification, type inference, existence checks.

title: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
code: as_polyglot_schema(schema)

title: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
text: schema schema specification (named list described ), NULL schema.

title: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
text: JSON string upstream ValidationSchema format, "" schema NULL. Mostly used internally; exported advanced users want inspect generated payload.

title: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
text: schema named list one entry per table. entry either: named character vector mapping column names SQL types, e.g. c(id = "INT", name = "TEXT"); unnamed character vector column names (types unknown), e.g. c("id", "name").

title: Specify a table schema for schema-aware operations — as_polyglot_schema
code: as_polyglot_schema(list(   orders = c(o_id = "INT", o_total = "DECIMAL(10,2)"),   users = c("id", "name") )) #> [1] "{\"tables\":[{\"name\":\"orders\",\"columns\":[{\"name\":\"o_id\",\"type\":\"INT\"},{\"name\":\"o_total\",\"type\":\"DECIMAL(10,2)\"}]},{\"name\":\"users\",\"columns\":[{\"name\":\"id\",\"type\":\"\"},{\"name\":\"name\",\"type\":\"\"}]}]}"

what: polyglotSQL: SQL Parsing, Analysis and Dialect Translation — polyglotSQL-package
title: polyglotSQL: SQL Parsing, Analysis and Dialect Translation — polyglotSQL-package
text: Parse, tokenize, validate, format, analyze translate SQL 30 dialects ('PostgreSQL', 'MySQL', 'BigQuery', 'Snowflake', 'DuckDB', 'T-SQL', others) using 'polyglot-sql' Rust crate https://github.com/tobilg/polyglot, Rust port 'SQLGlot' 'Python' library. processing happens locally R session; database connection, 'Python' runtime external service required. Includes column-level lineage, structural query analysis, query optimization, 'AST' diffing 'OpenLineage' facet generation.

title: polyglotSQL: SQL Parsing, Analysis and Dialect Translation — polyglotSQL-package
text: polyglotSQL embeds polyglot-sql Rust crate Tobias Müller (MIT), Rust port SQLGlot Toby Mao (MIT). See inst/COPYRIGHTS licenses vendored Rust dependencies.

title: polyglotSQL: SQL Parsing, Analysis and Dialect Translation — polyglotSQL-package

text: Versions polyglotSQL embedded Rust engine

title: Versions of polyglotSQL and its embedded Rust engine — polyglot_version
code: polyglot_version()

title: Versions of polyglotSQL and its embedded Rust engine — polyglot_version
text: named character vector elements polyglotSQL (R package version) polyglot_sql (version vendored polyglot-sql Rust crate package compiled ).

title: Versions of polyglotSQL and its embedded Rust engine — polyglot_version
code: polyglot_version() #>  polyglotSQL polyglot_sql  #>      "0.1.0"      "0.6.2"

title: Structural query analysis — sql_analyze
text: Extracts compact facts query: shape, output projections, referenced relations, CTEs, set operations star-projections.

title: Structural query analysis — sql_analyze
code: sql_analyze(sql, dialect = "generic", schema = NULL)

title: Structural query analysis — sql_analyze
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing. schema Optional schema specification (see as_polyglot_schema()); improves resolution unqualified ambiguous columns.

title: Structural query analysis — sql_analyze
text: polyglot_analysis object — list (among others): shape — query shape (e.g. "select", "setOperation"); projections — list per-output-column facts (name, transform kind, upstream column references, type hints schema given); relations — list referenced relations kind alias; ctes, cteFacts — CTE names per-CTE facts; setOperations, starProjections — present.

title: Structural query analysis — sql_analyze
code: a <- sql_analyze("WITH x AS (SELECT id FROM t) SELECT x.id, 2 AS two FROM x") a$shape #> [1] "select" vapply(a$projections, function(p) p$name, character(1)) #> [1] "id"  "two"

what: Annotate a query with inferred data types — sql_annotate_types
title: Annotate a query with inferred data types — sql_annotate_types
text: Runs upstream type inference AST. schema, column references resolve declared types; without one, types can inferred literals, casts function signatures filled.

title: Annotate a query with inferred data types — sql_annotate_types
code: sql_annotate_types(sql, dialect = "generic", schema = NULL)

title: Annotate a query with inferred data types — sql_annotate_types
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing. schema Optional schema specification (see as_polyglot_schema()); improves resolution unqualified ambiguous columns.

title: Annotate a query with inferred data types — sql_annotate_types
text: polyglot_ast object whose nodes carry inferred_type field type determined. Pass sql_generate() render, inspect $statements directly.

title: Annotate a query with inferred data types — sql_annotate_types
code: ast <- sql_annotate_types(   "SELECT id + 1 AS next_id FROM t",   schema = list(t = c(id = "INT")) ) # the Add node now carries inferred_type INT

title: List supported SQL dialects — sql_dialects
text: List supported SQL dialects

title: List supported SQL dialects — sql_dialects
code: sql_dialects(full = FALSE)

title: List supported SQL dialects — sql_dialects
text: full FALSE (default), return character vector canonical dialect names. TRUE, return data frame columns name, aliases (comma-separated accepted aliases) description.

title: List supported SQL dialects — sql_dialects
text: character vector, data frame full = TRUE.

title: List supported SQL dialects — sql_dialects
text: Every function takes dialect, argument accepts canonical names aliases (e.g. "postgresql" "postgres", "mssql" "sqlserver" "tsql").

title: List supported SQL dialects — sql_dialects
code: sql_dialects() #>  [1] "generic"     "postgresql"  "mysql"       "bigquery"    "snowflake"   #>  [6] "duckdb"      "sqlite"      "hive"        "spark"       "trino"       #> [11] "presto"      "redshift"    "tsql"        "oracle"      "clickhouse"  #> [16] "databricks"  "athena"      "teradata"    "doris"       "starrocks"   #> [21] "materialize" "risingwave"  "singlestore" "cockroachdb" "tidb"        #> [26] "druid"       "solr"        "tableau"     "dune"        "fabric"      #> [31] "drill"       "dremio"      "exasol"      "datafusion"  head(sql_dialects(full = TRUE)) #>         name  aliases                                    description #> 1    generic          Standard SQL with no dialect-specific behavior #> 2 postgresql postgres                                     PostgreSQL #> 3      mysql                                                   MySQL #> 4   bigquery                                         Google BigQuery #> 5  snowflake                                               Snowflake #> 6     duckdb                                                  DuckDB

what: Structural diff between two SQL statements — sql_diff
title: Structural diff between two SQL statements — sql_diff
text: Compares ASTs two statements reports inserted, removed, moved updated nodes.

title: Structural diff between two SQL statements — sql_diff
code: sql_diff(sql_from, sql_to, dialect = "generic", delta_only = TRUE)

title: Structural diff between two SQL statements — sql_diff
text: sql_from, sql_to Single SQL statements compare. dialect Dialect used parsing. delta_only TRUE (default), omit unchanged ("keep") nodes.

title: Structural diff between two SQL statements — sql_diff
text: data frame columns op ("insert", "remove", "move", "update", "keep"), expression (SQL affected node) target (updates, new SQL).

title: Structural diff between two SQL statements — sql_diff
code: sql_diff("SELECT a FROM t", "SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE a > 1") #>       op expression target #> 1 insert          a   <NA> #> 2 insert          b   <NA> #> 3 insert      a > 1   <NA> #> 4 insert          1   <NA> #> 5   move          a   <NA>

title: Format (pretty-print) SQL — sql_format
text: Parses re-renders SQL canonically indented statements. Complexity guards protect pathological inputs; exceeding guard raises polyglot_guard_error.

title: Format (pretty-print) SQL — sql_format
code: sql_format(   sql,   dialect = "generic",   max_input_bytes = NULL,   max_tokens = NULL,   max_ast_nodes = NULL,   max_set_op_chain = NULL )

title: Format (pretty-print) SQL — sql_format
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing rendering. max_input_bytes, max_tokens, max_ast_nodes, max_set_op_chain Complexity guard limits. NULL (default) uses upstream defaults (16 MiB input, 1e6 tokens, 1e6 AST nodes, 256 chained set operations); Inf disables guard; positive number sets explicit limit.

title: Format (pretty-print) SQL — sql_format
text: character vector one formatted statement per input statement.

title: Format (pretty-print) SQL — sql_format
code: cat(sql_format("select a,b from t where x=1 and y=2")) #> SELECT #>   a, #>   b #> FROM t #> WHERE #>   x = 1 AND y = 2

what: Generate SQL from a parsed AST — sql_generate
title: Generate SQL from a parsed AST — sql_generate
text: Renders sql_parse() result back SQL text using target dialect's syntax rules (keywords, quoting, literals). Note plain generation: unlike sql_transpile(), apply cross-dialect function rewrites (e.g. IFNULL converted COALESCE). Use render programmatically-built modified ASTs; use sql_transpile() full dialect translation.

title: Generate SQL from a parsed AST — sql_generate
code: sql_generate(ast, dialect = "generic")

title: Generate SQL from a parsed AST — sql_generate
text: ast polyglot_ast object sql_parse() (sql_annotate_types()). dialect Target dialect rendering.

title: Generate SQL from a parsed AST — sql_generate
text: character vector one element per statement AST.

title: Generate SQL from a parsed AST — sql_generate
code: ast <- sql_parse("SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1") sql_generate(ast, dialect = "postgres") #> [1] "SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1"

title: Column-level lineage — sql_lineage
text: Traces output column query back tables expressions derived , following CTEs, subqueries set operations.

title: Column-level lineage — sql_lineage
code: sql_lineage(sql, dialect = "generic", schema = NULL, column = NULL)

title: Column-level lineage — sql_lineage
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing. schema Optional schema specification (see as_polyglot_schema()); improves resolution unqualified ambiguous columns. column Optional single column name. default, lineage computed every output column query.

title: Column-level lineage — sql_lineage
text: polyglot_lineage object: list one entry per column, containing column — output column name; sources — character vector source tables feeding column; tree — lineage graph nested lists fields name, source_name, source_kind ("Table", "Cte", "DerivedTable", ...), expression (SQL node's expression) downstream.

title: Column-level lineage — sql_lineage
code: sql_lineage("SELECT a + b AS total FROM t") #> <polyglot_lineage> 1 column (generic) #> total ← t sql_lineage(   "WITH base AS (SELECT id, amount FROM payments)    SELECT id, amount * 2 AS doubled FROM base" ) #> <polyglot_lineage> 2 columns (generic) #> id ← payments #> doubled ← payments

title: OpenLineage column-lineage facet — sql_openlineage
text: Produces OpenLineage-compatible columnLineage facet plus inferred input/output datasets SQL statement, integration data catalogs lineage backends.

title: OpenLineage column-lineage facet — sql_openlineage
code: sql_openlineage(   sql,   dialect = "generic",   schema = NULL,   namespace = NULL,   job_name = NULL )

title: OpenLineage column-lineage facet — sql_openlineage
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing. schema Optional schema specification (see as_polyglot_schema()); improves resolution unqualified ambiguous columns. namespace Optional dataset namespace applied inferred datasets. job_name Optional job name recorded facet.

title: OpenLineage column-lineage facet — sql_openlineage
text: list elements facet (OpenLineage columnLineage facet), inputs, outputs (dataset descriptors) warnings.

title: OpenLineage column-lineage facet — sql_openlineage
code: ol <- sql_openlineage(   "INSERT INTO reports SELECT id, total FROM sales",   namespace = "warehouse" ) names(ol) #> [1] "facet"    "inputs"   "outputs"  "warnings"

title: Optimize SQL — sql_optimize
text: Applies upstream optimizer rule set (predicate pushdown, join reordering, CTE subquery elimination, expression simplification, etc.) returns rewritten SQL.

title: Optimize SQL — sql_optimize
code: sql_optimize(sql, dialect = "generic", schema = NULL)

title: Optimize SQL — sql_optimize
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing. schema Optional schema specification (see as_polyglot_schema()); improves resolution unqualified ambiguous columns.

title: Optimize SQL — sql_optimize
text: character vector one optimized statement per input statement.

title: Optimize SQL — sql_optimize
code: sql_optimize("SELECT * FROM (SELECT a FROM t) AS sub WHERE sub.a > 1") #> [1] "WITH _cte AS (SELECT a FROM t) SELECT * FROM _cte AS sub WHERE sub.a > 1"

what: Parse SQL into an abstract syntax tree — sql_parse
title: Parse SQL into an abstract syntax tree — sql_parse
text: Parse SQL abstract syntax tree

title: Parse SQL into an abstract syntax tree — sql_parse
code: sql_parse(sql, dialect = "generic")

title: Parse SQL into an abstract syntax tree — sql_parse
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing.

title: Parse SQL into an abstract syntax tree — sql_parse
text: polyglot_ast object: list elements statements — list one nested-list AST per statement; sql — input SQL; dialect — dialect used. AST node named list; name outer element gives node kind (e.g. "select"). structure follows upstream polyglot-sql JSON AST format round-trips sql_generate().

title: Parse SQL into an abstract syntax tree — sql_parse
code: ast <- sql_parse("SELECT a, b FROM t WHERE x = 1") ast #> <polyglot_ast> 1 statement (generic) #> [1] select names(ast$statements[[1]]) #> [1] "select"

what: List source tables referenced by SQL — sql_source_tables
title: List source tables referenced by SQL — sql_source_tables
text: Returns physical tables query reads , across statements. CTE names included (intermediate results, sources).

title: List source tables referenced by SQL — sql_source_tables
code: sql_source_tables(sql, dialect = "generic")

title: List source tables referenced by SQL — sql_source_tables
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing.

title: List source tables referenced by SQL — sql_source_tables
text: character vector table names, order first appearance.

title: List source tables referenced by SQL — sql_source_tables
code: sql_source_tables("SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.id") #> [1] "a" "b" sql_source_tables("WITH x AS (SELECT 1 FROM t) SELECT * FROM x") #> [1] "t"

title: Tokenize SQL — sql_tokenize
text: Splits SQL lexical tokens using tokenizer given dialect.

title: Tokenize SQL — sql_tokenize
code: sql_tokenize(sql, dialect = "generic")

title: Tokenize SQL — sql_tokenize
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing.

title: Tokenize SQL — sql_tokenize
text: data frame class polyglot_tokens one row per token: type (token type, e.g. "Select", "Identifier", "Number"), text (raw token text), line column (1-based position), start end (byte offsets, end exclusive).

title: Tokenize SQL — sql_tokenize
code: sql_tokenize("SELECT a FROM t") #> <polyglot_tokens> 4 tokens #>     type   text line column start end #> 1 Select SELECT    1      7     0   6 #> 2    Var      a    1      9     7   8 #> 3   From   FROM    1     14     9  13 #> 4    Var      t    1     16    14  15

title: Translate SQL between dialects — sql_transpile
text: Parses sql dialect regenerates dialect, rewriting functions, quoting, constructs needed (e.g. MySQL IFNULL() becomes PostgreSQL COALESCE()).

title: Translate SQL between dialects — sql_transpile
code: sql_transpile(   sql,   from,   to,   pretty = FALSE,   unsupported = c("raise", "warn", "ignore") )

title: Translate SQL between dialects — sql_transpile
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). Source dialect name (see sql_dialects()). Target dialect name (see sql_dialects()). pretty TRUE, pretty-print output. unsupported handle constructs represented target dialect: "raise" (default) throws polyglot_transpile_error; "warn" "ignore" continue return closest supported translation ("warn", upstream collects diagnostics still returns result).

title: Translate SQL between dialects — sql_transpile
text: character vector one element per input statement.

title: Translate SQL between dialects — sql_transpile
text: Transpilation syntactic best-effort: identical syntax can still behave differently across engines (implicit casts, collations, NULL ordering, integer division, time zone handling...). Always test translated SQL target database using production.

title: Translate SQL between dialects — sql_transpile
code: sql_transpile("SELECT IFNULL(a, b) FROM t", from = "mysql", to = "postgres") #> [1] "SELECT COALESCE(a, b) FROM t" sql_transpile(   "SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) FROM events",   from = "postgres", to = "duckdb" ) #> [1] "SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) FROM events"

title: Validate SQL — sql_validate
text: Checks SQL parses given dialect , optionally, applies stricter syntax rules, semantic lint warnings, schema-aware checks.

title: Validate SQL — sql_validate
code: sql_validate(sql, dialect = "generic", ...)

title: Validate SQL — sql_validate
text: sql single character string one SQL statements (separated ;). dialect Dialect used parsing. ... Additional validation options: strict_syntax — reject non-canonical syntax parser accept compatibility (e.g. trailing commas ); default FALSE. semantic — report query-quality warnings (W001–W004, e.g. SELECT * mixed explicit columns); default FALSE. schema — schema specification (see as_polyglot_schema()); enables unknown-table/column, type reference checks. error — TRUE, raise polyglot_validation_error instead returning invalid result; default FALSE.

title: Validate SQL — sql_validate
text: polyglot_validation object: list valid (logical) errors (data frame columns severity, code, message, line, column).

title: Validate SQL — sql_validate
code: sql_validate("SELECT a FROM t") #> <polyglot_validation> valid (generic) sql_validate("SELECT FROM WHERE") #> <polyglot_validation> invalid (generic) #> [E003] Expected table name or subquery, got Where at 1:18 v <- sql_validate("SELECT a, FROM t", strict_syntax = TRUE) v$valid #> [1] FALSE

title: polyglotSQL 0.1.0
text: Initial release, wrapping polyglot-sql Rust crate 0.6.2. Core: polyglot_version(), sql_dialects() (34 dialects aliases). Transpilation formatting: sql_transpile(), sql_format(), sql_generate() complexity guards configurable handling unsupported constructs. Parsing validation: sql_parse(), sql_tokenize(), sql_validate() (strict syntax, semantic warnings, schema-aware checks). Analysis lineage: sql_source_tables(), sql_lineage(), sql_analyze(), sql_optimize(), sql_diff(), sql_annotate_types(), sql_openlineage(). Classed error conditions (polyglot_error, polyglot_parse_error, polyglot_transpile_error, polyglot_validation_error, polyglot_guard_error, …); Rust failures never crash R session. Fully offline source builds via vendored crates (cargo vendor).
